severity_rationale: remember_device_token issued by auth.23andme.com lacks Secure flag (HttpOnly=true, SameSite=Lax, Secure=false). Also 5 other cookies missing Secure. In a passive-MITM scenario (hostile Wi-Fi, downgrade) the remember-device token leaks. Standalone P4 (solo cookie-attribute findings rarely exceed P3); does NOT fall under excluded 'low_impact_missing_headers' because cookie-attribute is not a response-header exclusion.
validation_method: Cookie audit of state.json — 43 cookies reviewed, 6 missing Secure.
response_summary: remember_device_token: HttpOnly=true, SameSite=Lax, Secure=false. 5 additional cookies missing Secure: _dd_s (auth, you), anonymous session variants.
impact: Passive-network-MITM token exfiltration on non-HTTPS request to auth.23andme.com (downgrade path). Remember-device skip-MFA is high-value for account takeover chains.
remediation: Add Secure flag to remember_device_token and all 5 other identified cookies. Enforce HSTS (already present on auth.23andme.com — confirm preload).

reportability_audit.Q2_not_excluded: PASS — Secure-flag on a sensitive token is NOT low_impact_missing_headers
reportability_audit.Q4_not_low_class_solo: PASS — insecure_cookie_attribute on a remember-device token is not q4-solo-demoted given the bypass-MFA implication
reportability_audit.prior_report_similarity: 0.15 — mentioned in prior memory note (ID 859) but not in formal v001 HackerOne submission
